title: Find Minimum in Rotated Array
description:
Given a rotated sorted array of distinct integers, find the minimum element. The array may have been rotated by an unknown amount.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^9
explanation:
Intuition
If the subarray is already sorted, the leftmost value is the minimum. Otherwise the pivot lies within. Compare mid to the endpoints to decide which side still contains the pivot and shrink accordingly.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Keep left and right indices.
2. Main Algorithm: If nums[left] ≤ nums[right], return nums[left]. Otherwise probe mid and move to the unsorted half.
3. Return Result: Track the best seen while shrinking.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(log n) because the search space halves at each step.
- Space Complexity: O(1) since only a few variables are maintained.

Key Insights
- A sorted interval implies its leftmost is minimal
- The pivot splits the array into two sorted parts

Common Pitfalls
- Using < vs ≤ changes behavior on two elements
- Confusing distinct elements with duplicates case
hints: Check if the interval is already sorted
